learning_outcomes:
BECEN_Learning Outcomes_ENG 1.  |  Major Programme:
Graduates from this Progamme will have acquired
(1)  |  acquired knowledge on understanding the central concepts, tools of
inquiry, and structures of early childhood education; (K/S)
(2)  |  acquired the ability to design curriculum and instructional plans
based on children's diverse abilities; (K/S)
(3)  |  applied a variety of instructional strategies to encourage the
development of curiosity, creativity, problem-solving and performance skills
in children; (K/S)
(4)  |  acquired knowledge and skills on a variety of formal and informal
assessment strategies, and to use various assessment strategies to evaluate
and ensure the continuous all-round development of young learners; (K/S/V)
(5)  |  demonstrated an understanding of the basic organizational and
managerial theories, and ways to adopt appropriate strategies to contribute,
independently and collegially, to the quality of their schools to the benefit
of children, parents, the community; (K/S/V)
(6)  |  recognized the complex characteristics of young children, young
children's families and communities, and to appreciate respectful and
reciprocal relationships that support children's development and learning; (K/S/V) and
(7)  |  developed themselves to be innovative reflective practitioners who
continually evaluate the effects of their choices and who actively research
and implement new methods and seek out opportunities to grow professionally.
(K/S/V)
K = Knowledge
outcomes S = Skills outcomes V = Values and
attitudes outcomes
study_scheme:
Early Childhood Education Early Childhood Education Applicable to students admitted in 2025-26
Major Programme Requirement
Students
are required to complete a minimum of 111 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
BECE2110[a], 2310[a], EDUC2240
2.  |  Required Courses:
Core Courses in Early Childhood
Education BECE2120, 2130, 2140, 2320, 2330, 2510, CHED4210,
4220, 4310, 4340, 4350, 4410, 4510, EDUC3201  |  40
Research Experience BECE4540[a], CHED4320  |  6
Teaching Practice BECE4010[a], 4020[a]  |  10
3.  |  Elective Courses:  |  46
46 units of courses chosen from (a), (b) and (c):
(a)  |  At least 26 units of courses chosen from the
following: BECE2210, 3110[a], 3120,
3160, 3170, 3210, 3220, 4341[a], CHED4330, 4530
(b)  |  At least 11 units of courses chosen
from the following: BECE3130, 3140, 3150, CHED4150,
4370, 4520, 4550, EDUC3150, 3200, 3280, 3290
(c)  |  A maximum of 9
units of courses chosen from the following: BMBL1001,
LING1000, 2003, 2004, 3201, 3204, 3208, MUSC1000, 2545, 2872, 2882, 3502,
3533, PSYC1000, 2010, 2300, 2620, SPED2141, 3410
Total:  |  111
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  BECE, CHED and EDUC courses at 2000 and above level will be
included in the calculation of Major GPA for honours classification.
[a]  |  Capstone courses
